top_signals.title: Mercuria shipping head says fuel shortages could idle 10% of global fleet
top_signals.summary: The shipping sector is fast approaching a fuel crisis that could paralyze a tenth of the global fleet, Larry Johnson, global head of freight at commodities trading house Mercuria, said in an interview. Since the Middle East war erupted, markets have been preoccupied with potential shortfalls in the diesel and jet fuel traditionally exported in ...

top_signals.title: Intra-Asia Container Index Up 10% Last Week
top_signals.summary: The Drewry Intra-Asia Container Index (IACI), the benchmark widely referenced by procurement teams, increased 10% to $1,114 per 40ft container, a record high, after strengthening in 11 of the last 12 weeks. top_signals.title: The Supreme Court Just Stripped Brokers of Their Biggest Legal Shield
top_signals.summary:
The unanimous decision in Montgomery v. Caribe Transport II eliminates federal preemption as a defense in negligent carrier selection lawsuits. J. J. Keller’s Josh Lovan sat down with FreightWaves to discuss how the ripple effects are already reshaping how freight moves.

alerts.title: SpaceX IPO Fuels Space Economy Buzz
alerts.summary: SpaceX is just days from Wall Street's biggest IPO ever. And as capital pours into the Space Economy, the opportunities go beyond rocketships. Space exploration yields critical infrastructure for plenty of Earth-bound industries, like agriculture and drug research. Ross Hamilton, the COO of Space Network and the author of "Everything is Flying" joined Bloomberg Open Interest to talk about how space technology already runs modern life on Earth and why a SpaceX IPO could reshape far more than the space industry. (Source: Bloomberg)
